TE Operational Flow Order -- UPDATE


Notice Text:

In order to maintain the operational integrity of the system, TE is updating the previously posted Operational Flow Order (OFO) pursuant to Section 4.3 of the General Terms and Conditions of TE's FERC Gas Tariff beginning 9:00 AM CCT January 22, 2026, to all OBA and Transportation Contracts, with the exception of those governed by a FERC gas tariff, in Texas Eastern's Market Area Zones M1-24, M1-30, M2-24, M2-30 and M3 as well as Access Area Zones STX, ETX, ELA and WLA.

 

This OFO does not affect the ability of TE to receive or deliver quantities of gas for scheduled nominations to any customer or pipeline.

 

The penalty for the Operational Flow Order will be equal to three times the daily Platts Gas Daily "Daily Price Survey" posting for the High Common price for the geographical region, as defined in Section 8.5(a) of the General Terms and Conditions of TE's FERC Gas Tariff for the day on which such violation occurred.  TE will not permit retroactive nominations to avoid an OFO penalty except for shippers who can acquire day ending Due Shipper supply.

 

Please refer to the table below for the effected TETCO zones, dates, and tolerance percentages.

Zone

Gas Day Jan 20

Gas Day Jan 21

Gas Day Jan 22

Gas Day Jan 23 and until further notice

M1-24"

No OFO

No OFO

M1-30"

No Due Pipe 4%/250

No OFO

No OFO

No OFO

 

NOTE:

Deliveries No DP : Shippers are not to take more than scheduled

Deliveries No DS : Shippers are not to take less than scheduled

Receipts No DP : Shippers are not to give less than scheduled

Receipts No DS : Shippers are not to give more than scheduled

 

TE may be required to issue an hourly OFO pursuant to General Terms and Conditions Section 4.3(H) to impose further restrictions in order to maintain the operational integrity of the system.

 

This OFO will remain in effect until further notice.

 

Please contact your Operations Account Representative should you have any questions.
